title: Tomorrow Outlook AI Output
description: Structured JSON output from the AI tomorrow outlook prompt. Provides forward-looking watchpoints and scheduled events for the next session. Validated by module 08 before publishing.

key_watchpoints.description: Key price levels, narratives, or conditions to watch tomorrow.

description.description: Why this watchpoint matters and what to look for.

source.description: Source that supports this watchpoint. Null when the watchpoint is derived from general context.

source_url.description: URL of the source item. Null when not available.

scheduled_events.description: Known scheduled events tomorrow that could move the market or affect the topic.

title.description: Event name (e.g. 'US CPI Release', 'Fed Chair Powell Speech').

time_hint.description: Optional time hint (e.g. '08:30 ET', 'Pre-market'). Null if time is not known.

source.description: Source for the scheduled event information. Null when the event is from general knowledge.

source_url.description: URL of the source item. Null when not available.

outlook_summary.description: 2–4 sentence forward-looking summary for the next session. Factual and grounded in the day's events.

risk_level.description: Overall perceived risk or uncertainty level for tomorrow.

key_watchpoints.title: Bitcoin $120K Support Hold
key_watchpoints.description: After breaking above $120K, holding this level as support is critical for continued momentum. A failure to hold could trigger a pullback to $115K.

key_watchpoints.title: Spot ETF Flow Continuation
key_watchpoints.description: Sustained inflows above $1B would confirm institutional conviction. A reversal below $500M could dampen sentiment.

examples.outlook_summary: Bitcoin enters Friday's session at all-time highs with strong momentum. The key risk tomorrow is macro: the FOMC minutes could introduce volatility if language around rate cuts shifts. ETF flows will be closely watched for any sign of profit-taking by institutional players.

key_watchpoints.title: Fed Chair Speech — Rate Guidance Tone
key_watchpoints.description: After a hotter-than-expected CPI print, Chair Powell's remarks tomorrow could move markets materially if he signals a shift in rate-cut timing.

key_watchpoints.title: US 10-Year Yield Direction
key_watchpoints.description: The 10-year yield rose sharply after the CPI beat. Whether it holds above 4.5% may influence equity valuations, particularly in rate-sensitive sectors.

key_watchpoints.title: Services Inflation Follow-Through
key_watchpoints.description: The re-acceleration in services CPI could prompt repricing across fixed income. Watch for analyst revisions to the Fed's projected rate path.

examples.outlook_summary: Markets may remain cautious ahead of the Fed Chair's speech, with the hotter CPI print raising questions about the pace of rate cuts. Treasury yields and the US dollar could see continued volatility. Retail sales data may offer additional clues on consumer resilience, though the primary focus is likely to remain on Fed communication.
